audit_items.proof_trace: counter.h defines three counter classes (BasicCounter, AtomicCounter, AtomicCounter64) whose sole data member is a value-type integer (`uint32_t _counter` or `uint64_t _counter`), never a pointer. All operations either do arithmetic directly on `_counter` or pass `&_counter` (address of a member variable, always non-null) to atomic helper functions in atomic.h. There are zero pointer dereferences in this file where a null pointer could appear.
